mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held flat or declining relative to its 3-month average lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing a persistent tailwind for S&P 500 prices over the next month regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and forward 20d SPX return stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or positive-rate-trend periods do not show statistically higher mean returns (p > 0.05) than negative-trend periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate consistent with a Sahm-rule-style trigger signals an incipient recession, causing risk premia to widen and S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter to turn negative as earnings expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp above its 3-month trailing average
failure_condition: In more than 50% of historical instances where the trigger fires, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive, or the average post-trigger return is not significantly below the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth and housing starts both accelerate simultaneously, the joint signal reflects broad-based consumer and investment demand strength that is not captured by either series alone, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as growth expectations improve.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth > 0 and HOUST 3-month growth > 0 concurrently
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances produce forward 20d returns statistically indistinguishable from (or below) baseline unconditional returns, or the interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC versus either series individually (delta IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects healthy real-side capacity utilization, translating into modest positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week as production data confirm ongoing economic momentum.
failure_condition: The correlation between trailing INDPRO growth and forward 5d SPX return is not distinguishable from zero (|corr| < 0.02) across a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, when CPI inflation remains elevated while the fed funds rate continues rising, the combination signals sustained restrictive policy intent, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rising for 3+ consecutive months AND CPIAUCSL YoY change > 3%
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated inflation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during joint-trigger periods are not significantly negative relative to baseline (p > 0.05), or the interaction effect fails to outperform the FEDFUNDS-only trend hypothesis by at least 0.02 Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls exceeding a meaningful threshold signals an abrupt labor market contraction, prompting a negative repricing of equity risk over the subsequent month as recession probability rises.
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward SPX return following triggered payroll declines is not statistically different from the unconditional mean (p > 0.05), or fewer than 55% of triggered instances show negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing downcycle regime, characterized by declining housing starts over multiple quarters, further deterioration in HOUST amplifies recession fears more strongly than in normal regimes, producing outsized negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: During confirmed multi-month HOUST declines, 60-day forward SPX returns are not significantly more negative than during non-declining periods (p > 0.05), invalidating the regime amplification claim.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, when industrial production growth decelerates while payroll growth remains strong, the divergence signals labor hoarding ahead of a production slowdown, foreshadowing a short-term equity pullback within the next week as markets anticipate margin compression.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month growth decelerating while PAYEMS 3-month growth remains positive and stable
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following the divergence trigger are not significantly negative relative to baseline (p > 0.05), or the interaction signal underperforms a naive INDPRO-only trend model by more than 0.02 Rank IC.
